faqs.a: It's difficulty managing the intensity, duration, or timing of emotions. Feelings may arrive faster than expected, hit harder than the situation seems to call for, and take longer to settle. It's a feature of several conditions rather than a diagnosis by itself.

It shows up across ADHD, borderline personality disorder, PTSD and other trauma-related conditions, and mood disorders, and it can also follow significant stress or sleep loss in people without any diagnosis. A clinician looks at what's driving it, since the right support depends on the cause.
Yes. Regulation is a set of skills that tends to improve with practice and support. Skills-based therapies such as DBT teach people to notice, slow, and steer strong emotions over time.
